segments.text: Now the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to the children of Israel, and say to them:
When a man consecrates by a vow certain persons to the Lord, according to your valuation,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: if your valuation is of a male from twenty years old up to sixty years old, then your valuation shall be fifty shekels of silver, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If it is a female, then your valuation shall be thirty shekels;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: and if from five years old up to twenty years old, then your valuation for a male shall be twenty shekels, and for a female ten shekels;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: and if from a month old up to five years old, then your valuation for a male shall be five shekels of silver, and for a female your valuation shall be three shekels of silver;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: and if from sixty years old and above, if it is a male, then your valuation shall be fifteen shekels, and for a female ten shekels.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But if he is too poor to pay your valuation, then he shall present himself before the priest, and the priest shall set a value for him; according to the ability of him who vowed, the priest shall value him.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If it is an animal that men may bring as an offering to the Lord, all that anyone gives to the Lord shall be holy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He shall not substitute it or exchange it, good for bad or bad for good; and if he at all exchanges animal for animal, then both it and the one exchanged for it shall be holy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If it is an unclean animal which they do not offer as a sacrifice to the Lord, then he shall present the animal before the priest;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: and the priest shall set a value for it, whether it is good or bad; as you, the priest, value it, so it shall be.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But if he wants at all to redeem it, then he must add one-fifth to your valuation.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And when a man dedicates his house to be holy to the Lord, then the priest shall set a value for it, whether it is good or bad; as the priest values it, so it shall stand.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If he who dedicated it wants to redeem his house, then he must add one-fifth of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall be his.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If a man dedicates to the Lord part of a field of his possession, then your valuation shall be according to the seed for it. A homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If he dedicates his field from the Year of Jubilee, according to your valuation it shall stand.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee, then the priest shall reckon to him the money due according to the years that remain till the Year of Jubilee, and it shall be deducted from your valuation.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And if he who dedicates the field ever wishes to redeem it, then he must add one-fifth of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall belong to him.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But if he does not want to redeem the field, or if he has sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed anymore;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: but the field, when it is released in the Jubilee, shall be holy to the Lord, as a devoted field; it shall be the possession of the priest.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And if a man dedicates to the Lord a field which he has bought, which is not the field of his possession,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: then the priest shall reckon to him the worth of your valuation, up to the Year of Jubilee, and he shall give your valuation on that day as a holy offering to the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought, to the one who owned the land as a possession.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And all your valuations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs to the shekel.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But the firstborn of the animals, which should be the Lord's firstborn, no man shall dedicate; whether it is an ox or sheep, it is the Lord's.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And if it is an unclean animal, then he shall redeem it according to your valuation, and shall add one-fifth to it; or if it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Nevertheless no devoted offering that a man may devote to the Lord of all that he has, both man and beast, or the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed; every devoted offering is most holy to the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: No person under the ban, who may become doomed to destruction among men, shall be redeemed, but shall surely be put to death.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the tree, is the Lord's. It is holy to the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If a man wants at all to redeem any of his tithes, he shall add one-fifth to it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And concerning the tithe of the herd or the flock, of whatever passes under the rod, the tenth one shall be holy to the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He shall not inquire whether it is good or bad, nor shall he exchange it; and if he exchanges it at all, then both it and the one exchanged for it shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: These are the commandments which the Lord commanded Moses for the children of Israel on Mount Sinai.
